title: ProTECT Act of 2025
plain_summary: The ProTECT Act of 2025 would make it illegal to hunt endangered and threatened animals for trophies in the United States. It would also ban people from bringing trophy parts like heads or skins from these protected animals into the country from other places. Right now, the government can sometimes give special permission for trophy hunting of threatened animals, but this bill would stop that completely. The law would protect both endangered species, which are animals that might disappear forever, and threatened species, which are animals that could become endangered soon.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Prohibiting Threatened and Endangered Creature Trophies Act of two thousand twenty-five or the ProTECT Act of two thousand twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. First, trophy hunting of animals that are in danger of disappearing hurts efforts to save wildlife populations. This is because trophy hunters usually target the biggest and strongest male animals. When these animals are removed from their group, it can reduce genetic variety, lower reproduction rates, change how animal groups are organized, increase the killing of baby animals, and cause unnatural changes in how the species evolves. Second, when trophy hunting of endangered animals is allowed by law, illegal hunting also increases. This further threatens the survival of wildlife populations. Third, legal trade in wildlife parts and products can hide illegal trade in wildlife products. This illegal trade is worth up to twenty billion dollars every year and is run by professional criminal networks. These same networks are also involved in other organized crimes including trafficking in drugs, weapons, and humans. Fourth, while the Endangered Species Act of nineteen seventy-three currently prohibits taking and importing species listed as endangered species, it does not automatically give those same protections to species listed as threatened species.

sections.title: Section 3 Prohibitions Regarding Taking and Importing of Endangered Species and Threatened Species Trophies
sections.text: This section changes the Endangered Species Act in several ways. First, it adds new rules that make it illegal for any person under United States law to kill for a trophy any threatened fish or wildlife species within the United States or its territorial waters, or to bring into the United States any trophy from any threatened fish or wildlife species. Second, it changes the permit rules so that the Secretary cannot give permits or otherwise allow anyone to kill for a trophy any endangered or threatened fish or wildlife species, or to import into the United States any trophy from any endangered or threatened fish or wildlife species. Third, it changes the antique rules so that the new trophy import ban also applies to antiques, with an exception. Fourth, it adds a new definition of trophy. A trophy means a whole dead animal or a part or product made from an animal that can be easily recognized as coming from that animal. The animal part or product can be raw, processed, or manufactured. It must have been obtained under a hunting license or other permission given by any state, foreign government, or private landowner.
